make this button solid blue color and the label of the button should pass the contrast test after that @Ahmed Marwan

job.title: Make ENQUIRE NOW button solid blue with contrast-safe label

jobs.commentText: Why don't we try to make this light mode instead of dark mode @Ahmed Marwan

options.label: Full inversion
options.caption: I swapped the whole page over: backgrounds move to Tone/50, body copy to Tone/900, and I kept Amber/500 as the single accent so the page still reads as the same brand, just lit differently.
options.aiPrompt: On the 'landing page' frame, replace all background fills currently using dark Tone/Navy paint styles with Tone/50, set all body and heading text color styles to Tone/900, and keep any Amber accent fills unchanged. Do not hardcode hex values — apply existing local paint styles only.

options.label: Warm ivory + navy text
options.caption: Instead of a flat white swap, I set the backgrounds to Tone/100 for a warmer paper feel and pulled text color to Navy/900 so headlines stay grounded rather than going flat black-on-white.
options.aiPrompt: On the 'landing page' frame, set section background fills to the Tone/100 paint style and set heading and label text color styles to Navy/900. Leave body copy on Tone/700 for hierarchy. Use only existing local paint styles, no hex values.

options.label: Split hero/body
options.caption: I kept the Hero + Nav dark for drama on arrival, then dropped everything below it — Brand Intro, stats, form — to light mode, so the page opens bold and settles into an airy, easier-to-read scroll.
options.aiPrompt: On the 'landing page' frame, leave the 'Hero + Nav' section's existing dark paint styles untouched. For every 'Brand Intro' frame/instance below it, change background fills to Tone/50 and text color styles to Tone/900, using only existing local styles.

report.author: Ahmed Marwan
report.comment: change this text to "Serviced Portfolio" @Ahmed Marwan
report.rationale: Unambiguous copy swap on a specific text layer identified by the comment pin.

report.author: Ahmed Marwan
report.comment: make this button solid blue color and the label of the button should pass the contrast test after that @Ahmed Marwan
report.rationale: Swapping the button fill to a blue paint style and setting the label's text color to a contrasting style are both deterministic, declarative ops.
report.jobTitle: Make ENQUIRE NOW button solid blue with contrast-safe label

report.author: Ahmed Marwan
report.comment: Why don't we try to make this light mode instead of dark mode @Ahmed Marwan
report.rationale: Switching the whole landing page from dark to light mode requires visual judgment across paint styles, not a deterministic op list.
